channel.title: Morning Briefing for George
channel.description: Personalized daily briefing covering business, markets, and industry news. Single-listener feed.
channel.itunes:author: George Cherian
channel.itunes:summary: Personalized daily briefing covering business, markets, and industry news. Single-listener feed.

item.title: Briefing — Tuesday, June 30
item.description: In world business, the European Central Bank's inflation outlook is darkening. Bundesbank president Joachim Nagel told CNBC on the sidelines of an ECB conference in Portugal that inflation is likely…

item.title: Briefing — Monday, June 29
item.description: In world business, the Bank for International Settlements — the central bank of central banks — is sounding an alarm about AI-driven market froth. Its annual report flags rich stock valuations…

item.title: Briefing — Friday, June 26
item.description: In world business, the Federal Reserve remains a split signal. Chicago Fed President Goolsbee said Thursday that inflation is still too high, while New York Fed President Williams struck a more…

item.title: Briefing — Thursday, June 25
item.description: In world business, the big story is memory chips. Micron reported quarterly revenue of forty-one point four six billion dollars — more than quadruple the nine point three billion it posted a year…

item.title: Briefing — Wednesday, June 24
item.description: In world business, the White House says it will investigate potential price gouging by oil companies. Global crude prices have fallen since the US-Israel conflict with Iran, but remain elevated…

item.title: Briefing — Tuesday, June 23
item.description: In world business, oil prices are falling as investors grow cautiously optimistic that Middle East tensions may ease. Citi analysts are describing the geopolitical risk premium as an "overhang" that…

item.title: Briefing — Monday, June 22
item.description: In world business, Treasury yields are climbing as markets return from the holiday weekend. The ten-year note rose more than three basis points to four point four eight three percent, with investors…

item.title: Briefing — Thursday, June 18
item.description: In world business, the Federal Reserve under new Chairman Kevin Warsh held rates steady at Wednesday's meeting and notably stripped the cutting bias from its policy statement — a signal that markets…

item.title: Briefing — Wednesday, June 17
item.description: In world business, UK inflation held steady at two point eight percent in May, according to the Office for National Statistics. Food price increases slowed enough to offset higher petrol costs. The…

item.title: Briefing — Monday, June 15
item.description: In world business, a US-Iran deal announced over the weekend is moving markets. President Trump confirmed the Strait of Hormuz will be reopened under the agreement — oil prices dropped on the news…

item.title: Briefing — Friday, June 12
item.description: In world business, Meta has reportedly begun unwinding its two billion dollar acquisition of Manus, the Chinese-founded AI startup, after Beijing ordered the deal reversed. It's an unusual assertion…

item.title: Briefing — Thursday, June 11
item.description: In world business, US inflation hit four point two percent in May — a three-year high and the third consecutive monthly increase since the start of the Iran conflict. The closure of the Strait of…

item.title: Briefing — Wednesday, June 10
item.description: In world business, U.S. military strikes against Iran are moving oil markets this morning. Prices rose on concerns that renewed conflict could threaten shipping through the Strait of Hormuz — a…

item.title: Briefing — Tuesday, June 9
item.description: In world business, the New York Fed's latest consumer survey shows household financial worries have hit their highest level since July twenty twenty-two. The inflation outlook held mostly steady…

item.title: Briefing — Monday, June 8
item.description: In world business, the Middle East is moving markets this morning. Iran announced an end to its latest military operation against Israel, which sent oil prices retreating after they briefly topped…

item.title: Briefing — Friday, June 5
item.description: In world business, energy markets have been volatile enough this year that quantitative hedge funds — the computer-driven trend-followers — have made significant gains riding the swings. Those funds…

item.title: Briefing — Thursday, June 4
item.description: In world business, Asia-Pacific markets opened lower overnight as renewed tensions between Iran and the U.S. raised concerns about sustained inflation pressure. Separately, Amazon engineers in…

item.title: Briefing — Wednesday, June 3
item.description: In world business, the U.S. Trade Representative has proposed fresh tariffs on sixty economies, citing forced labor trade practices. The proposal sets a ten percent duty rate for economies with full…

item.title: Briefing — Tuesday, June 2
item.description: In world business, the Trump administration is proposing a twenty-five percent tariff on Brazilian goods under a Section 301 investigation, citing unfair trade practices. That adds Brazil to a…

item.title: Briefing — Monday, June 1
item.description: In world business, Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell is sounding an alarm about political pressure from the White House, warning it will erode public trust in the central bank's independence…
